# block — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The block feature owns the actor-to-actor safety relationship within VCSM. It allows citizens to block and unblock other actors, enforcing bidirectional content filtering across feed, chat, notifications, and profile surfaces. All mutations are RPC-backed through the `moderation.*` schema with dual-enforcement trust boundaries (app-layer `assertingActorId` assertion + RPC-side `is_current_vc_actor` guard). The feature is fully self-contained with no shared engine dependency.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/block/
**Engine Path:** None — feature-only
**Security Tier:** HIGH
**Feature Status:** ACTIVE
**Total Source Files:** 18

---

## Layer Presence

| Layer | Present | Path |
|---|---|---|
| Controllers | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/block/controllers/ |
| DALs | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/block/dal/ |
| Models | NO | None — no transform/model layer present |
| Hooks | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/block/hooks/ |
| Screens | NO | None — block surfaces are embedded in profiles/settings/chat |
| Components | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/block/ui/ + guards/ |
| Adapters | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/block/adapters/ |
| Engine controllers | NO | None |
| Engine DALs | NO | None |

---

## Active Controllers

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| `blockActor.controller.js` | Exports `blockActorController`, `unblockActorController`, `toggleBlockActorController` — block/unblock orchestration with idempotency checks | `assertingActorId === blockerActorId` assertion thrown on mismatch; `checkBlockStatus()` pre-check guard before every write |
| `getBlockStatus.controller.js` | Exports `ctrlGetBlockStatus` — bidirectional block status read wrapping `checkBlockStatus` DAL | Null-safe self-check guard: returns `{ isBlocked: false }` for self or missing actors |
| `getBlockedActorSet.controller.js` | Exports `ctrlGetBlockedActorSet` — bulk blocked-actor resolution for feed filtering and friend ranking | Input validation only — no session assertion (read-only, non-mutating) |

No dead or superseded controllers identified. Note: `blockActor.controller.js` previously contained `invalidateFeedBlockCache` call; moved to hook layer (SF-01, 2026-05-14) per SENTRY finding.

---

## Active DALs

| DAL | Tables / RPCs | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `block.check.dal.js` | `moderation.blocks` (SELECT, `.eq("status", "active")`) | Bidirectional check — returns `{ isBlocked, blockedByMe, blockedMe }`; uses `isUuid()` guard; returns safe defaults for invalid input |
| `block.read.dal.js` | `moderation.blocks` (SELECT bulk OR query) | Bulk read via `.or()` clause; returns `Set<string>` of blocked actor IDs; performance path for feed filtering and friend ranking |
| `block.write.dal.js` | RPC: `moderation.block_actor`, `moderation.unblock_actor` | Write path is RPC-only — never direct table INSERT; RPCs are `SECURITY DEFINER` and handle follow deactivation, friend_ranks cleanup, and block_event audit row atomically |

**Additional DAL note:** A `dev/diagnostics/dal/block.diagnostics.dal.js` exists for dev-only diagnostics (`fetchActorsIBlocked`, `fetchActorsWhoBlockedMe`, `fetchBlockGraph`) — moved from production DAL per SENTRY SF-02 (2026-05-14). Not part of production code path.

**Satellite DAL drift (documented, not in this feature):** `features/settings/privacy/dal/blocks.dal.js`, `features/notifications/inbox/dal/blocks.read.dal.js`, and `features/feed/pipeline/feed.read.blockRows.dal` each maintain their own block reads outside the block feature barrel — documented as architectural drift; consolidation deferred.

---

## Active Hooks

| Hook | What It Calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `useBlockActions.js` | `blockActorController`, `unblockActorController` (controllers); `useIdentity` (identity adapter); `invalidateFeedBlockCache` (feed adapter) | Block/unblock lifecycle management with loading + error state; calls `invalidateFeedBlockCache` post-mutation (SF-01 fix) |
| `useBlockActorAction.js` | `blockActorController` (controller); `useIdentity` (identity adapter); `invalidateFeedBlockCache` (feed adapter) | Single-action block callback — lighter than `useBlockActions`; used by feed actions |
| `useBlockStatus.js` | `ctrlGetBlockStatus` (controller) | Block status lifecycle with cancellation guard; derives `canViewProfile`, `canInteract` booleans |

**Adapter hooks (thin re-exports):**
- `adapters/hooks/useBlockActorAction.adapter.js` — re-exports `useBlockActorAction`
- `adapters/hooks/useBlockStatus.adapter.js` — re-exports `useBlockStatus`

---

## Engine Dependencies

None — feature-only. Block interacts with Supabase directly via `@/services/supabase/supabaseClient` and `@/services/supabase/postgrestSafe`. No `engines/` import paths present in any block feature file.

**Note:** `BlockedState.jsx` imports `useActorSummary` from `@hydration` (hydration engine alias) for actor display. This is a UI-only display dependency — not a business logic dependency.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What Is Imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| `features/identity` | `useIdentity` from `@/features/identity/adapters/identity.adapter` | Block → Identity (inbound: session actor resolution) |
| `features/feed` | `invalidateFeedBlockCache` from `@/features/feed/adapters/feedCache.adapter` | Block → Feed (outbound: cache invalidation side effect after block mutation) |

Both imports are via adapter surfaces — correctly bounded. No direct internal feature imports.

**Consumers of block (outbound — via barrel or adapters):**
- `features/social/friend/` — consumes `ctrlGetBlockStatus`, `ctrlGetBlockedActorSet`
- `features/profiles/` — consumes `useBlockStatus`, `ctrlGetBlockedActorSet`, `BlockGate`
- `features/feed/` — consumes `useBlockActorAction` adapter
- `features/chat/` — consumes `useBlockStatus` adapter
- `features/settings/privacy/` — consumes `ctrlUnblockActor` (via barrel)
- `features/upload/` — consumes `ctrlGetBlockedActorSet`

---

## Authorization Pattern

The block feature uses a **dual-enforcement session-binding pattern**:

**App-layer (controller):**
All mutating controllers (`blockActorController`, `unblockActorController`, `toggleBlockActorController`) require three arguments: `blockerActorId`, `blockedActorId`, and `assertingActorId`. The first guard in every mutating controller is:
```
if (!assertingActorId || assertingActorId !== blockerActorId) throw Error
```
This ensures the session actor (provided by `useIdentity()` in calling hooks) matches the actor performing the block. Self-block is rejected at both controller and DAL levels. Unblock additionally verifies `blockedByMe === true` via `checkBlockStatus()` before calling the RPC — ensuring the caller owns the block edge.

**RPC-layer (server-side):**
Both `moderation.block_actor` and `moderation.unblock_actor` are `SECURITY DEFINER` RPCs with `is_current_vc_actor(p_actor_id)` ownership verification. Server-side enforcement is independent of the app layer.

**RLS (2026-05-10 migration):**
`blocks_select_blocked` policy allows the blocked actor to read rows where they are the target — required for bidirectional block enforcement in post visibility queries.

**Trust boundary verdict (VENOM 2026-05-11):** SOLID. Zero CRITICAL findings. 1 HIGH open (VF-01 — friend_ranks cleanup gap).

---

## Module Independence Classification

**MOSTLY INDEPENDENT**

Reason: The feature's controllers, DALs, hooks, and components are fully self-contained within `apps/VCSM/src/features/block/`. Its only runtime dependencies on other features are via adapters (`identity` for session resolution, `feed` for cache invalidation) — both correctly bounded. The feature is consumed widely by profiles, chat, feed, notifications, and settings, but those consumers access block only through its adapter or barrel surfaces.

---

## Architecture State

**STABLE**

The block system has completed SENTRY, VENOM, CARNAGE, KRAVEN, IRONMAN, and THOR governance passes. All SENTRY findings (SF-01 through SF-03) and VENOM findings (VF-02 through VF-05) are resolved. The architecture is actor-first, RPC-backed, and adapter-bounded. Remaining open items (VF-01, LF-01, LF-02) are DB-level or performance concerns, not architecture violations. The source structure is clean and consistent.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **VF-01 (HIGH — OPEN):** `vc.friend_ranks` rows are NOT cleaned up after a block. Blocked actors may surface in friend suggestions. Requires batch4 migration (`20260510100000_fix_block_actor_bidirectional_follows.sql`) deployment plus historical orphan backfill. `applyBlockSideEffects.js` helper marked dead pending this migration.

2. **LF-01 (MEDIUM — OPEN):** `useBlockStatus` is called without caching on profile load — duplicate uncached `checkBlockStatus` reads cause read amplification (2.0x). No memoization or SWR cache layer present.

3. **LF-02 (LOW — OPEN):** `invalidateFeedBlockCache` is present in `useBlockActions.js` but absent from `useBlockActorAction.js` — feed cache may not invalidate when block is triggered via the action hook path.

4. **Satellite DAL drift (MEDIUM — DEFERRED):** Three other features (`settings/privacy`, `notifications/inbox`, `feed/pipeline`) maintain their own block read DALs outside the block feature barrel. This creates duplicate query paths and risks divergence if block schema changes. Consolidation deferred.

5. **FALCON P0 gaps (BLOCKED — OPEN):** Three iOS native parity gaps (NTB-03, NTB-02, NDF-01) remain unverified. THOR is BLOCKED for iOS native and Android.

6. **Zero test coverage:** No feature-owned test files exist. SPIDER-MAN has not run. TESTS.md is derived from THOR release findings only.

7. **`BlockButton.jsx` imports `useIdentity` from `@/state/identity/identityContext`** — a non-adapter direct import. Other hooks correctly use `@/features/identity/adapters/identity.adapter`. This is an inconsistency requiring alignment.

---

## Recommended Handoffs

| Command | Reason |
|---|---|
| **SPIDER-MAN** | Zero automated test coverage. Block is HIGH security tier — unit and integration tests needed for controller auth gates, idempotency paths, and bulk filter. |
| **CARNAGE** | batch4 migration (`20260510100000`) not yet deployed. `vc.friend_ranks` cleanup gap (VF-01) is the primary open HIGH finding. |
| **ELEKTRA** | Has not run. Precision security scan recommended for the `blockActor.controller.js` argument chain and RPC ownership verification. |
| **HAWKEYE** | Has not run. Endpoint and API contract verification for block-aware query surfaces. |
| **LOGAN** | Documentation gap: Logan docs not confirmed. `zNOTFORPRODUCTION/_CANONICAL/logan/` entry for block system should be confirmed or created. |

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

Core architecture (controllers, DALs, hooks, adapters, components) is fully present and correctly layered. Trust boundary is solid. The feature is stable and actively consumed by profiles, chat, feed, notifications, and settings. Open blockers are DB-level (VF-01, batch4 migration) and coverage gaps (SPIDER-MAN not run, FALCON P0 gaps for iOS native).
